test: FX interface Line B (anchored vs floating) — FIRST real-data pass
data: user FRED files DEXHKUS, DEXJPUS (daily 2021-07..2026-07, aggregated monthly)
interpretation: A rule-anchored currency (HKD board) shows ~12x lower FX volatility vs USD than a free float (JPY) over the same window. Direction and magnitude consistent with the model's core hypothesis: fixing one side's nominal anchor removes a large share of bilateral-rate variance.
honest_caveats: TWO currencies only — a demonstration, not a publishable result. Real strength needs the full anchored/floating panel (8-14 currencies) from the protocol.

HKD is a HARD PEG, the low-volatility BRACKET. A CS currency floats nominally while anchoring inflation, so it sits BETWEEN peg and free-float — this pass shows the bracket's low end, not the CS point.
Short window (2021-2026, n~60 months) that happens to include the 2022 JPY slide — a single stress episode inflates the floating vol. Longer sample needed.
Line A (variance decomposition into PPP/UIP/residual) NOT run — needs HK + Japan CPI and policy rates, not uploaded. This pass is Line B only.
No causal identification: HKD's low vol is partly the peg mechanism itself, not purely an inflation anchor. Bracket logic, not a clean CS estimate.
verdict: Consistent with the model at a first pass; clearly labeled preliminary. Upgrades to a real result when the full currency panel + CPI + rates are added.
